title: sida cordata
intro: Sida cordata, also known as the water stork's bill or water sida, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Sida cordata include reducing fever, relieving cough and cold, and treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies like poultices and ointments.
